root_cause: Disk exhaustion on /var/log: unrotated log files and PostgreSQL WAL accumulation consumed all available disk space. No disk usage alert was configured below 95%.
resolution_steps: Immediate: `find /var/log -name '*.log' -mtime +7 -delete` to free space

output:

RESOLUTION STEPS:

5. Increase volume size or add log shipping to object storage

  Seq Scan on audit_logs (cost=0.00..480994.00 rows=48099485)
    Filter: ((created_at > '2024-01-01') AND (user_id = 12345))
    Rows Removed by Filter: 48,099,385
  Planning time: 2.3ms, Execution time: 79226ms
root_cause: Missing composite index on audit_logs(user_id, created_at): full sequential scan of 48,099,485 rows. Recent table growth exceeded threshold where seq scan becomes costlier than index scan.
resolution_steps: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_audit_logs_user_created ON audit_logs(user_id, created_at DESC)

Schedule regular ANALYZE on high-growth tables
Consider partitioning large tables by created_at

  at notification-service/handlers/catalog.go:89
root_cause: Cache stampede on key 'homepage_data': when TTL (300s) expires, thousands of concurrent requests simultaneously attempt DB reload, overwhelming the database. No mutex or probabilistic early expiration implemented.
resolution_steps: Implement mutex/single-flight pattern: only one goroutine rebuilds cache

Monitor cache hit rate; alert if drops below 90%

SERVICE: notification-service
SEVERITY: high

LOGS:
[2025-10-13T18:39:03Z] ERROR notification-service: Timeout calling payment-gateway after 5000ms
[2025-10-13T18:39:03Z] WARN  notification-service: Circuit breaker HALF-OPEN for payment-gateway
[2025-10-13T18:39:03Z] ERROR notification-service: payment-gateway health check failed (6 consecutive failures)
[2025-10-13T18:39:03Z] WARN  notification-service: Circuit breaker OPEN for payment-gateway – failing fast
[2025-10-13T18:39:03Z] ERROR notification-service: Request queue backing up (641 pending)
[2025-10-13T18:39:03Z] CRIT  notification-service: Cascading failure – upstream services timing out

METRICS:
Downstream payment-gateway error rate: 82%, Circuit breaker: OPEN, Thread pool: 92% saturated (waiting on payment-gateway), Request success rate: 12%, Bulkhead queue depth: 277

ERROR TRACE:
ConnectTimeout: failed to connect to payment-gateway:9130 after 5000ms
  at notification-service/clients/payment_gateway.py:89 (call)
  at resilience4j/circuit_breaker.py:234
  Caused by: payment-gateway TCP connect timeout
  Retry attempts: 3/3 exhausted
output:
ROOT CAUSE: Cascading timeout failure: payment-gateway is down/slow, causing notification-service threads to block waiting for responses. Thread pool saturation then causes failure cascade to upstream callers. Circuit breaker opened after 4 failures.

CONFIDENCE: 91%

RESOLUTION STEPS:
1. Investigate payment-gateway independently (separate on-call rotation if external)
2. Verify circuit breaker is correctly shedding load (check breaker state)
3. Implement bulkhead isolation: dedicated thread pool for {downstream} calls
4. Add fallback: return cached/default response when payment-gateway is unavailable
5. Review timeout values – 30s may be too long if downstream is unresponsive

root_cause: Rate limit breach against google-maps: recent feature deployment triggered notification fan-out without rate limiting, sending 20000/min against a 10000/min limit. No rate limiting or token bucket implemented.
resolution_steps: Implement token bucket rate limiter for google-maps client

Consider upgrading google-maps plan if usage is legitimately higher

metrics: CPU: 99%, Single-thread CPU: 100%, Request queue: 1262, Throughput: dropped 69%, Context switches: 213/sec (very low – thread blocked)
error_trace:
Thread dump – consumer-0 state: RUNNABLE for >300s
  at api-gateway.processing.DataProcessor.parseEvent(DataProcessor.java:445)
  at api-gateway.processing.DataProcessor.processLoop(DataProcessor.java:312)
  at java.util.regex.Pattern.compile(Pattern.java:1891)

root_cause: CPU hot-loop in api-gateway: catastrophic regex backtracking in DataProcessor.parseEvent(). Pathological input triggers O(2^n) regex evaluation, pinning consumer-0 at 100% CPU.
resolution_steps: Replace backtracking regex with linear-time alternative or possessive quantifiers
Add regex timeout using Pattern.matcher with timeout (Java 21+)
Validate and sanitise input before regex application
Add CPU spike alert: >90% for >2 minutes triggers page
Consider ReDoS testing in CI pipeline

metrics: Disk /var/lib/postgresql: 98% used, Available: 443MB, Write IOPS: 0 (blocked), Log file growth: +1821MB/day, WAL files: 29GB
error_trace:
OSError: [Errno 28] No space left on device: '/var/lib/postgresql/app.log.2025-04-10'
  at api-gateway/logging/file_handler.py:78
  at logging/handlers.py:120 (emit)
PostgreSQL FATAL: could not write to file in pg_wal
root_cause: Disk exhaustion on /var/lib/postgresql: unrotated log files and PostgreSQL WAL accumulation consumed all available disk space. No disk usage alert was configured below 95%.
resolution_steps: Immediate: `find /var/lib/postgresql -name '*.log' -mtime +7 -delete` to free space
Configure logrotate with daily rotation and 7-day retention
Set disk alert at 80% (warning) and 90% (critical)
If WAL: check replication slots blocking WAL cleanup (`pg_replication_slots`)
Increase volume size or add log shipping to object storage

instruction: You are an expert SRE. Analyze the following production incident and identify the root cause, confidence level, and step-by-step resolution.
input:
SERVICE: payment-service
SEVERITY: high

LOGS:
[2025-10-14T23:21:28Z] INFO  redis: Cache miss for key 'homepage_data' (TTL expired)
[2025-10-14T23:21:28Z] WARN  payment-service: 4485 concurrent cache misses for 'homepage_data'
[2025-10-14T23:21:28Z] ERROR postgres: connection pool exhausted (stampede origin)
[2025-10-14T23:21:28Z] WARN  payment-service: DB query queue depth: 719 (normal: <10)
[2025-10-14T23:21:28Z] ERROR payment-service: Request latency spike to 16937ms
[2025-10-14T23:21:28Z] INFO  redis: Cache rebuilt for 'homepage_data' after 9s

METRICS:
Cache hit rate: dropped from 98% to 0% at 2025-10-14T23:21:28Z, DB QPS spike: 1222x normal, Redis memory: stable (miss not eviction), API error rate: 52% during stampede, Recovery time: 29s

ERROR TRACE:

  3233 goroutines racing to rebuild cache
  DBError: connection pool exhausted during rebuild
  at payment-service/cache/manager.go:156 (GetOrSet)
  at payment-service/handlers/catalog.go:89
output:
ROOT CAUSE: Cache stampede on key 'homepage_data': when TTL (600s) expires, thousands of concurrent requests simultaneously attempt DB reload, overwhelming the database. No mutex or probabilistic early expiration implemented.

CONFIDENCE: 81%

RESOLUTION STEPS:
1. Implement mutex/single-flight pattern: only one goroutine rebuilds cache
2. Use probabilistic early expiration (XFetch algorithm) for hot keys

4. Add cache warming job before TTL expiry for predictable keys
5. Monitor cache hit rate; alert if drops below 90%

  DBError: connection pool exhausted during rebuild
  at search-service/cache/manager.go:156 (GetOrSet)
  at search-service/handlers/catalog.go:89
root_cause: Cache stampede on key 'price_list': when TTL (300s) expires, thousands of concurrent requests simultaneously attempt DB reload, overwhelming the database. No mutex or probabilistic early expiration implemented.
resolution_steps: Implement mutex/single-flight pattern: only one goroutine rebuilds cache

Monitor cache hit rate; alert if drops below 90%

root_cause: Cache stampede on key 'product_catalog': when TTL (300s) expires, thousands of concurrent requests simultaneously attempt DB reload, overwhelming the database. No mutex or probabilistic early expiration implemented.

resolution_steps: Immediate: `find /var/lib/postgresql -name '*.log' -mtime +7 -delete` to free space

Increase volume size or add log shipping to object storage

output:

Monitor cache hit rate; alert if drops below 90%

  Planning time: 2.3ms, Execution time: 117919ms
root_cause: Missing composite index on audit_logs(user_id, created_at): full sequential scan of 49,370,643 rows. Recent table growth exceeded threshold where seq scan becomes costlier than index scan.

Consider partitioning large tables by created_at

  DBError: connection pool exhausted during rebuild
  at order-service/cache/manager.go:156 (GetOrSet)
  at order-service/handlers/catalog.go:89
root_cause: Cache stampede on key 'user_session': when TTL (600s) expires, thousands of concurrent requests simultaneously attempt DB reload, overwhelming the database. No mutex or probabilistic early expiration implemented.
resolution_steps: Implement mutex/single-flight pattern: only one goroutine rebuilds cache

Monitor cache hit rate; alert if drops below 90%
